title: Pycreus betschuanus
taxonomy: Pycreus betschuanus is a species of sedge, classified as a member of the family Cyperaceae and the genus Pycreus. It is a type of grass-like plant found in various parts of the world. The classification of Pycreus betschuanus is further categorized into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. This classification places Pycreus betschuanus as a flowering plant with a monocotyledonous nature. Its classification is also reflected in its inclusion in the APG III system of plant classification.

It is traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
